text_excerpt: In Parliament | Laura Trott MP Skip to main content Main navigation About Laura Local Plan In Parliament Contact News Residents survey In Parliament Tweet You can find all my formal contributions to debates in Parliament here. I am working hard both inside and outside the House of Commons chamber to represent the interests of Sevenoaks & Swanley residents - so if there is something you’d like me to raise, please get in touch by emailing [email protected] - please be sure to include your full name and address. Friend is always correct to be worried, but he will know that in Lord Nash’s amendment a 12-month delay was written in. Friend is always correct to be worried, but he will know that in Lord Nash’s amendment a 12-month delay was written in. After Clause 26 - Power to require internet service providers to prevent or restrict access by children to internet services | Children’s Wellbeing and Schools Bill | Commons debates On Friday, after Labour MPs had repeatedly blocked a social media ban for under-16s, we had a new proposal from the Government. After Clause 26 - Power to require internet service providers to prevent or restrict access by children to internet services | Children’s Wellbeing and Schools Bill | Commons debates I would be incredibly concerned if that were the case. After Clause 26 - Power to require internet service providers to prevent or restrict access by children to internet services | Children’s Wellbeing and Schools Bill | Commons debates As ever, the hon. Gentleman is correct. I am sure that will be picked up by the Minister in her closing remarks. After Clause 26 - Power to require internet service providers to prevent or restrict access by children to internet services | Children’s Wellbeing and Schools Bill | Commons debates My right hon. Friend, as ever, is absolutely correct. As on the smartphone ban in schools, we have been fought every step of the way.
